content.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Now let's begin coordination. My feature only touches `outlines/types/__init__.py`.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "PROPOSE" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "" --note "Adding hash_sha256 type to outlines/types/__init__.py only, appending near end of file. Small addition, shouldn't conflict unless you're also editing this file - let me know your files."

coop-send agent1 --type "ACCEPT" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Agreed: you add ssn after isbn definition. I'll append hash_sha256 at the very end of the file (after all existing definitions) to keep insertion points disjoint. Will re-read the file right before editing to pick up your changes first."

content.text: Both ACCEPTs exchanged. Now implementing — I'll read the current file state and append `hash_sha256` at the end.

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive)

content.text: Now let's verify the implementation works correctly.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256

valid = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'[:64]
print('valid len 64:', len(valid), hash_sha256.matches(valid))

import re
# full match test using pattern directly
pattern = hash_sha256.pattern
print('pattern:', pattern)

tests = {
    'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85': True,  # 64 lower
    'E3B0C44298FC1C149AFBF4C8996FB92427AE41E4649B934CA495991B7852B85': True,  # 64 upper
    'e3B0c44298FC1c149afBF4c8996fb92427AE41e4649b934CA495991b7852B85': True,  # mixed
    'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b8':  False, # 63 chars
    'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855': False, # 65 chars
    '': False,
    'g3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85': False, # invalid char g
    ' e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85': False, # leading space
}
for s, expected in tests.items():
    m = re.fullmatch(pattern, s)
    ok = bool(m) == expected
    print(len(s), repr(s[:20]), 'expected', expected, 'got', bool(m), 'OK' if ok else 'MISMATCH')
"

input.command:
python3 -c "
h='e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'
print(len(h))
import hashlib
print(hashlib.sha256(b'').hexdigest(), len(hashlib.sha256(b'').hexdigest()))
"

python3 -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256
import re

h = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'
print('len', len(h))
pattern = hash_sha256.pattern
tests = {
    h: True,
    h.upper(): True,
    'e3B0c44298FC1c149afBF4c8996fb92427AE41e4649b934CA495991b7852B855': True,
    h[:-1]: False,   # 63 chars
    h + 'a': False,  # 65 chars
    '': False,
    'g' + h[1:]: False,
    ' ' + h[1:]: False,
    h + ' ': False,
}
for s, expected in tests.items():
    m = re.fullmatch(pattern, s)
    ok = bool(m) == expected
    print(len(s), repr(s[:15]), 'expected', expected, 'got', bool(m), 'OK' if ok else 'MISMATCH')

print()
print('matches() method (may not anchor):')
for s, expected in tests.items():
    print(len(s), hash_sha256.matches(s), 'expected', expected)
"

from outlines.types import hash_sha256
from pydantic import BaseModel

class FileRecord(BaseModel):
    filename: str
    checksum: hash_sha256

schema = hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None)
print(schema)
r = FileRecord(filename='a.txt', checksum='e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855')
print(r)
opt = hash_sha256.optional()
print(type(opt))
m = hash_sha256.at_least(1)
print(type(m))
"

)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive)
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

content.text:
The patch is written to `patch.txt` and contains only the intended change: a new `hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")` type appended to `outlines/types/__init__.py`, which inherits `Regex`'s full DSL/Pydantic integration.

Verified behavior:
- Matches valid 64-char hex strings (lower/upper/mixed case)
- Rejects 63 or 65 char strings, empty strings, non-hex chars, and whitespace
- `hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__` returns `{"type": "string", "pattern": "..."}`
- Works as a Pydantic field type and supports DSL ops (`.optional()`, `.at_least()`)

Coordinated with agent1 via the handshake protocol — we agreed to a disjoint insertion point in the same file (they added `ssn` after `isbn`; I appended `hash_sha256` at the end) to avoid clobbering.

- Matches valid 64-char hex strings (lower/upper/mixed case)
- Rejects 63 or 65 char strings, empty strings, non-hex chars, and whitespace
- `hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__` returns `{"type": "string", "pattern": "..."}`
- Works as a Pydantic field type and supports DSL ops (`.optional()`, `.at_least()`)
